meaning: v.挫败,阻挠,使感到灰心,阻止
examples: Words frustrate her, like fastened buttons at which she can only fumble.
Gender inequality continues to frustrate a lot of female employees.
First, choose problems to work on that "challenge but do not frustrate".
Investors have worried that recent sharp gains in energy costs could frustrate the global economic recovery.
They know there is an increased risk of regulatory intervention by Ofcom to frustrate the takeover.
